Lotus Technology is currently building, and expects to continue to develop, its headquarters in Wuhan, on land over which it has acquired land use right certificates. rewritten: What is the main business of Lotus Technology?
pos_recalls: the Joint Venture Agreement of Lotus Company between the Transferor and the Transferee “Business” The business of Lotus Technology is to engage in activities within the automotive industry and related sectors that do not require public permits, as well as to provide advisory and other services, particularly in rendering research, production, and development activities. Lotus Technology is also engaged in research and development of automobiles and key components, as well as automotive engineering development services.

Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

Lotus Technology Inc. (“the Company”), an exempted company with limited liability, was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, consolidated variable interest entity (“VIE”) and VIE’s subsidiaries (collectively, “the Group”), is primarily engaged in the design, development, and sale of luxury battery electric lifestyle vehicles (“BEV lifestyle models”) under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus BEV business”). The Group is also a distributor that sells luxury sports cars under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus sports car business”) across the world. Additionally, the Group provides other sales and ancillary services to customers.

Lotus Technology has a limited operating history, and its ability to develop, manufacture, and deliver automobiles of high quality and appeal to customers, on schedule, and on a large scale is unproven and still evolving. Lotus Technology's electric vehicle (EV) business was founded in 2018. The company's first vehicle model, the Eletre, was launched in 2022. There is limited historical basis for making judgments on the demand for Lotus Technology's vehicles or the company's ability to develop, manufacture, and deliver vehicles, or its profitability in the future. It is difficult to predict Lotus Technology's future revenues and appropriately budget for its expenses, and the company may have limited insight into trends that may emerge and affect its business. The sustainability of Lotus Technology's business depends, in large part, on the company's ability to timely execute its plan to develop, manufacture, and deliver on a large scale automobiles of high quality and appeal to customers. Lotus Technology has entered into an agreement with Geely Holding to manufacture its new models for the global market using the Wuhan manufacturing facility.

Lotus Technology is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance lifestyle electric vehicles and has set high quality and safety standards for and makes continuous improvements on its vehicles and technologies, including sensory hardware and ADAS software. Lotus Technology also plans to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing its intellectual properties to other luxury auto brands and providing ADAS software subscriptions to its customers. Continue to launch new models and upgrade existing models. The successful launches of new models are critical for Lotus Technology to continue capturing market share in the luxury BEV market and strengthening its leadership position. Lotus Technology plans to launch a well-balanced portfolio of new models with exceptional performance in the coming years to broaden its customer base, in particular high net worth individuals and the tech-savvy younger generations, and expand its product breadth. Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully-electric vehicles over the next two years, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026. Lotus Technology also intends to upgrade its models to equip its vehicles with innovative technology and design. Focus on sustainability and lead in electrification.

Master Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK Pursuant to the Distribution Agreement entered into by and between Lotus Cars Limited, the entity carrying out Lotus UK’s sportscar manufacturing operations, and LTIL, Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where LTIL will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Cars Limited. In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus achieved a 105% year-on-year increase in topline growth, attributed to growing deliveries. Sales of goods reached $624 million, reflecting a 104% year-on-year increase. Service revenues also surged by 129% year-on-year, totaling $29 million and making up 4.4% of total revenue, an increase from 3.9% in the same period of 2023. Proactive inventory management strategies resulted in a temporary impact on gross margin, but this was partially offset by the growth of the high-margin intelligent driving business. The gross margin of sales of goods declined to 7% for the first nine months of 2024, compared to 10% in the same period of 2023, mainly due to destocking. However, the gross margin of service revenues saw an increase to 55% from 28% during the same period, driven by high-margin technology-related income. The company focused on optimizing operations and organizational structures to improve efficiency while maintaining customer experience.

"Lotus Technology continued to deliver strong operational growth, with total deliveries of 7,617 units in the first nine months of 2024, up by 136% year-on-year, driven by the European market," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "Lotus Technology is excited to see significant progress in its intelligent driving business across the world, with revenue from customers other than Lotus surging to \$11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%, contributing about 2% of total revenue. Lotus Technology also launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology, in line with evolving market demands. The company remains committed to executing the ‘Win26’ Plan to drive efficiency and competitiveness, delivering sustainable value for its shareholders and investors." "Lotus Technology has made continual improvements and streamlined its operations to drive efficiency, optimize resources, and achieve a reduction in operating expenses for four consecutive quarters," said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer.

As a result of the foregoing, Lotus Technology's gross profits increased from US$6.1 million for the six months ended June 30, 2023, to US$50.9 million for the six months ended June 30, 2024. Lotus Technology's gross margin increased from 4.7% for the six months ended June 30, 2023, to 12.8% for the six months ended June 30, 2024, primarily due to (i) the cost reduction per vehicle and increased average selling price of vehicles sold; and (ii) the increase in income from intelligent driving solutions, commission service fees, and other automotive design and development services to OEM customers, which carry higher margins.

The contract value distribution by region for the same period revealed that 70% originated from China, while Europe and the rest of the world (ROW) accounted for the remaining 30%.

Why is the sales volume in China low?China is a new market for Lotus. In the first half of this year, China contributed 25% of the brand's deliveries, while historically, China contributed only about 5% of Lotus's sales. In the context of a sharp decline in sales of luxury brands in China during the first half of this year, Lotus still achieved double-digit sales growth in the Chinese market. China is an important market for Lotus. Lotus will continue to enhance brand awareness in China and strengthen services to increase sales. ⚫ The main reason for the low gross margin is the increase in the cost of the Emira sports car due to inflation, as well as seasonal changes in the product sales mix. ⚫ Lotus's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus has taken a series of measures to respond to market changes (see below).

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

"Lotus Technology continued to deliver strong operational growth, with total deliveries of 7,617 units in the first nine months of 2024, up by 136% year-on-year, driven by the European market," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "Lotus Technology is excited to see significant progress in its intelligent driving business across the world, with revenue from customers other than Lotus surging to \$11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%, contributing about 2% of total revenue. Lotus Technology also launched Hyper Hybrid EV technology, in line with evolving market demands. The company remains committed to executing the ‘Win26’ Plan to drive efficiency and competitiveness, delivering sustainable value for its shareholders and investors." "Lotus Technology has made continual improvements and streamlined its operations to drive efficiency, optimize resources, and achieve a reduction in operating expenses for four consecutive quarters," said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer.

Deliveries of lifestyle vehicles saw a remarkable 126% year-on-year increase in the first nine months of 2024, constituting 52% of the total deliveries. Europe, being the home of the Lotus brand, demonstrated significant growth with a 372% year-on-year increase in vehicle deliveries during this period. The Rest of the World (ROW) region achieved a 110% year-on-year increase, as Lotus continued to expand into new markets. Furthermore, the company has surpassed 200 stores worldwide, with per-store sales steadily improving through operational optimization. The delivery breakdown for the first nine months of 2024 was 52% lifestyle vehicles and 48% sportscars. By region, deliveries were distributed as follows: Europe accounted for 35%, China for 25%, North America for 18%, and ROW for 22%. Regarding the store distribution, Europe held 34%, China had 25%, North America had 24%, and ROW had 17%.

question: 哪些市场已经在销售Eletre？
rewritten: Which markets are already selling the Eletre?
pos_recalls: The global luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, as defined by BEVs with a manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP) of over US$80,000, is expected to grow rapidly. However, the global luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022. The company expects to capture market share and realize first mover advantages by addressing unfilled demands in the current market. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt EPA. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed. Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum of 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds.

Lotus Technology offers three different versions of Eletre, namely, Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R, to satisfy the various demands of customers. Eletre R, in particular, generates a maximum 905 horsepower (hp) and can accelerate from 0 to 100 km/h in 2.95 seconds. Its 112-kWh battery pack offers a maximum WLTP range of 490 km and can be recharged from 10% to 80% in less than 20 minutes. While offering unrivaled performance, Eletre comes at a better value-for-money proposition — with an average MSRP higher than US$100,000 — compared to traditional luxury original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Deliveries of Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Planning is underway for deliveries to the U.S. and the rest of the world. Lotus Technology's second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and deliveries of Emeya began in March 2024. In addition to Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully electric vehicles in the near future, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026.

Lotus Technology has started deliveries of the Eletre to new markets, including the Gulf Cooperation Council region, New Zealand, India, and Egypt, following deliveries in Southeast Asia. Production orders for the Eletre have begun for new markets such as Australia, Japan, and Korea. Lotus Technology also started deliveries of the Emeya in Europe and is taking reservations for the Emeya in the Gulf Cooperation Council region, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. The company managed to maintain an average selling price of over US$100,000 in the first half of 2024, despite faster-than-sector growth and intensified competition. Lotus Technology has assessed evolving market conditions and uncertainties posed by new tariff policies in the U.S. and EU, and the company has revised its delivery target for 2024 to 12,000 units. Lotus Technology launched the “Win26” plan, aiming to achieve positive EBITDA and positive operating cash flow in 2026 by further optimizing its internal processes and structures, implementing overall cost control measures, and recalibrating its product plans to cater to globally diversified markets.

How is the company's overseas expansion situation?As a legendary brand with a history of 76 years, Lotus has a high reputation in the global market. The company currently has over 200 sales outlets in key global locations. The company's global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries. The company's lifestyle vehicles (Eletre and Emeya) are gradually entering new markets. After delivery in Southeast Asia, the Eletre SUV has started delivery in markets such as the Middle East, New Zealand, India, and Egypt. Eletre SUV production orders have commenced in Australia, Japan, and South Korea. The Emeya GT sedan has started delivery in Europe and has been pre-ordered in the Middle East, Northeast Asia, and Southeast Asia.

Lotus Technology is a luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer that designs, develops, and sells luxury lifestyle vehicles under the iconic British brand “Lotus.” With over seven decades of heritage and proven leadership in racetrack and road cars, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. The first lifestyle production vehicle, Eletre, is a new breed of pure electric SUV powered by Lotus’s proprietary 800-volt electric powertrain architecture (EPA). Deliveries of the Eletre commenced in China and Europe in 2023. Planning is underway for deliveries to the U.S. and the rest of the world. The second lifestyle production vehicle, Emeya, was launched in September 2023, and deliveries of the Emeya began in March 2024. In addition to the Eletre and Emeya, Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully electric vehicles in the near future, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV sports car in 2026. Lotus Technology also plans to upgrade its models on an ongoing basis.

Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

On November 11, 2021, Lotus Technology issued 95,990,886 ordinary shares to Lotus Technology International Investment Limited, which is ultimately 100% owned by Geely Holding. Through a series of reorganization steps (the “Reorganization”), including transferring the assets and employees in the Lotus BEV business unit of Ningbo Geely R&D into Wuhan Lotus Cars and transferring the equity of Wuhan Lotus Cars to the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), Lotus Technology gained control over the WFOE through Lotus HK on December 15, 2021. The equity interests of Lotus Technology UK and Lotus GmbH were also transferred to the Group on December 29, 2021, and June 24, 2022, respectively. On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology entered into trademark license agreements with a related party, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited (“LGIL”), which is ultimately controlled by Mr. Li Shufu. Pursuant to this agreement, Lotus Technology received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as Lotus Technology conducts business in relation to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars). Lotus Technology issued 47,995,443 ordinary shares as consideration for such trademark licenses. The above Reorganization was completed on June 24, 2022.

rewritten: Does Lotus Tech own the Lotus brand?
pos_recalls: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology Inc. entered into trademark license agreements with a related party (“Trademark License Agreements”), Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of LGIL, which is ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder of Lotus Technology Inc. Pursuant to the Trademark License Agreements, Lotus Technology Inc. received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as Lotus Technology Inc. conducts business in relation to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars): i) exclusively on lifestyle vehicles and parts and components related to those lifestyle vehicles that Lotus Technology Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, assembles, distributes, and sells or has designed, developed, manufactured, or assembled by any third party; ii) non-exclusively for Lotus Technology Inc.’s business of providing related after-sale services for the lifestyle vehicles; iii) non-exclusively on related products (excluding anything relating to sports cars) such as accessories that Lotus Technology Inc. designs, produces, distributes, and sells or has designed or produced by any third party on its behalf; iv) any other occasions in relation to the business conducted by Lotus Technology Inc. as duly approved by Lotus Technology Inc.’s Board (excluding anything relating to sports cars).

The equity interests of Lotus Tech UK and Lotus GmbH were transferred to Lotus Technology Corporation on December 29, 2021, and June 24, 2022, respectively. On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology Corporation entered into trademark license agreements with a related party, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited, or LGIL, pursuant to which Lotus Technology Corporation received the “Lotus” trademark licenses as long as it conducts business related to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars). Lotus Technology Corporation issued 216,700,000 ordinary shares as consideration for such trademark. The above reorganization was completed on June 24, 2022. On November 12, 2021, the former Variable Interest Entity (VIE) and a third party established Ningbo Robotics Co., Ltd., or Ningbo Robotics, in which the former VIE held a 60% equity interest. In March 2022, the former VIE transferred its 60% legal equity interest in Ningbo Robotics to its then wholly-owned subsidiary, Sanya Lotus Venture Capital Co., Ltd. On March 15, 2022, Lotus Technology Corporation (LTC) declared a 10-for-1 stock split in the form of a stock dividend, which was distributed to all the shareholders of LTC in proportion to their respective shareholdings in LTC.

On November 11, 2021, Lotus Technology issued 95,990,886 ordinary shares to Lotus Technology International Investment Limited, which is ultimately 100% owned by Geely Holding. Through a series of reorganization steps (the “Reorganization”), including transferring the assets and employees in the Lotus BEV business unit of Ningbo Geely R&D into Wuhan Lotus Cars and transferring the equity of Wuhan Lotus Cars to the Wholly Foreign-Owned Enterprise (WFOE), Lotus Technology gained control over the WFOE through Lotus HK on December 15, 2021. The equity interests of Lotus Technology UK and Lotus GmbH were also transferred to the Group on December 29, 2021, and June 24, 2022, respectively. On November 4, 2021, Lotus Technology entered into trademark license agreements with a related party, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Group International Limited (“LGIL”), which is ultimately controlled by Mr. Li Shufu. Pursuant to this agreement, Lotus Technology received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as Lotus Technology conducts business in relation to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars). Lotus Technology issued 47,995,443 ordinary shares as consideration for such trademark licenses. The above Reorganization was completed on June 24, 2022.

On November 4, 2021, Group Lotus Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of LGIL, which is ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder of the Company, entered into trademark license agreements with a related party (“Trademark License Agreements”). Pursuant to the Trademark License Agreements, the Group received the “Lotus” trademark licenses for as long as the Group conducts business related to lifestyle vehicles (excluding sports cars): i) exclusively for lifestyle vehicles and parts and components related to those vehicles that the Group designs, develops, manufactures, assembles, distributes, and sells, or has designed, developed, manufactured, or assembled by any third party; ii) non-exclusively for the Group’s business of providing related after-sale services for lifestyle vehicles; iii) non-exclusively for related products (excluding anything relating to sports cars), such as accessories that the Group designs, produces, distributes, and sells, or has designed or produced by any third party on its behalf; iv) any other occasions related to the business conducted by the Group as duly approved by the Group’s Board (excluding anything relating to sports cars). The Group intends to market and distribute its products under the “Lotus” brand indefinitely, and the trademark licenses are expected to contribute to cash flows indefinitely.

Lotus Technology Inc. (“the Company”), an exempted company with limited liability, was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, consolidated variable interest entity (“VIE”) and VIE’s subsidiaries (collectively, “the Group”), is primarily engaged in the design, development, and sale of luxury battery electric lifestyle vehicles (“BEV lifestyle models”) under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus BEV business”). The Group is also a distributor that sells luxury sports cars under the “Lotus” brand (“Lotus sports car business”) across the world. Additionally, the Group provides other sales and ancillary services to customers. New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

Additional mergers and acquisitions in the electric vehicle and luxury automotive markets may result in even more resources being concentrated in these competitors. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, its focus on design and experience, its advanced technologies, its relationship with Geely Holding and therefore the benefits it can obtain during the vehicle development phase, and its future-proof approach give it a competitive edge and allow it to formulate a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that it believes generates significant competitive advantages, allowing it to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance sports car brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker.

If competitors introduce new cars or services that successfully compete with or surpass the quality or performance of Lotus Technology's cars or services at more competitive prices, the company may be unable to satisfy existing customers or attract new customers at such prices and levels, and its business, financial condition, results of operations, and prospects will be affected. Lotus Technology's reliance on a variety of arrangements with Geely Holding, such as agreements related to research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, and IT and human resource-related supporting services, could subject the company to risks. Lotus Technology has entered into a variety of agreements with its strategic partner, Geely Holding, including agreements related to research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, and IT and human resource-related supporting services. The company's reliance on these agreements subjects it to a number of significant risks, including the risk of being unable to operate as a standalone business, launch new vehicles, reach its development and production targets, or focus its efforts on core areas of differentiation. Of particular importance for Lotus Technology's operations are the related party agreements with Geely Holding and its affiliate entities.

Leveraging Geely Holding’s readily available production capacity, Lotus Technology believes it can execute its business plan with higher scalability and flexibility while limiting its upfront capital commitments compared to most other original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Additionally, by leveraging Geely Holding’s global supply chain network, strong bargaining power in procurement, and stable relationships established with reputable suppliers such as NVIDIA, Qualcomm, and CATL, Lotus Technology can secure high-quality components at more competitive prices, which it believes would allow for better management of any supply chain disruption risk more effectively compared to other OEMs. Lotus Technology brings customers a luxury retailing experience through a digital-first, omni-channel sales model to establish and develop direct relationships with customers, covering the entire spectrum of customer experience, both physically and virtually. Lotus Technology operates premium stores in high-footfall locations, providing personalized and exclusive services to create a luxurious purchasing experience for its customers. The global sales digital platform provides a full suite of luxury retailing experiences, including a virtual showroom of the Lotus brand and products, an inquiry, order, purchasing and customization platform, and a reservation system for test driving, product delivery, and aftersales services, among others.

There is a risk of potential disputes with Geely Holding, and Lotus Technology could be affected by adverse publicity related to Geely Holding whether or not such publicity is related to their collaboration. Lotus Technology's ability to successfully build a luxury lifestyle vehicle brand could also be adversely affected by perceptions about the quality of Geely Holding’s vehicles. In addition, although Lotus Technology is involved in each step of the supply chain and manufacturing process, given that Lotus Technology also relies on Geely Holding to meet its quality standards, there can be no assurance that Lotus Technology will successfully maintain quality standards. If Lotus Technology is unable to maintain collaboration and partnership with Geely Holding, Lotus Technology may be unable to enter into new agreements with third-party manufacturing partners on terms and conditions acceptable to Lotus Technology or at all. Lotus Technology's ability to operate as a standalone business, produce vehicles, reach development and production targets, or focus efforts on core areas of differentiation could be materially and adversely affected. Additionally, Lotus Technology generated a portion of revenue from automotive design and development services provided to Geely Holding.

Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a SPAC affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable the company to effectively raise brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family.
